name: security-research
tagline: Orchestrates a 5-agent Team Mode audit (3 vuln hunters + 2 PoC engineers) to find and prove exploitable vulnerabilities in a repo or diff.
verdict: Clean, well-scoped skill: it explicitly bans destructive exploits against real/third-party systems, requires local fixtures/dry-runs, and produces a strict evidence-based report. Minor gaps only: no explicit negative-use guidance and no version field in frontmatter. Safe to install; it audits code, it does not exfiltrate or execute untrusted remote code.

highlights.summary: No explicit 'do not use for X' guidance anywhere in the file; a user could invoke it for non-security tasks with no steering away.

Relies on opaque team categories ('deep', 'ultrabrain', 'unspecified-high', lines 45-49) whose behavior is defined by the external oh-my-openagent platform, not this file, so audit depth/determinism v
Frontmatter has no version field and the file has no changelog/change-signal, so consumers can't detect updates between installs.
